title: Configuring the Web Environment on Windows
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on Microsoft Windows
Configuring the Web Environment on Windows
text: Configuring the Web Environment on Windows Uniface provides a complete web deployment environment for testing and deploying web applications and web services. The default installation includes a fully-configured Apache Tomcat HTTP Server and servlet engine. However, after installation, you can configure another web server and servlet engine to work with Uniface. To configure your own web environment for Uniface, you need: J2EE-compliant web server, which can run Java servlets as J2EE web containers. Uniface Web Application Server (WASV), consisting of the Uniface Server, the Uniface Router, and the Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD). Note: The following procedure is not required for the default web test environment. To configure the web environment: Configure the web server and servlet engine to run Uniface servlets. For more information, see Configure a Web Server for Uniface Applications . Configure the Uniface Web Application Server. For more information, see Configuring the Uniface Web Application Server . If you want to run the Tomcat servlet engine under Microsoft IIS, see Configuring Microsoft IIS to Use Tomcat . Related Links Deploying Uniface Web Applications The Uniface default installation includes a fully-configured Apache Tomcat HTTP Server and servlet engine for testing and deploying Uniface web applications. For production deployment, you can configure another web server and servlet engine to run Uniface applications after installation. Deploying a Uniface Web Service Was this topic helpful?
